transcript:
Agent: SecureBank customer security line, Marcus speaking. How can I help you today?

Customer: Hi Marcus. I just got an email alert about a login from a device I do not recognize in another state. I did not log in from there.

Agent: Thank you for calling immediately, Sandra. This is exactly the right thing to do. I am going to secure your account right now. First, let me verify your identity. Can you confirm the last four digits of your account number and your date of birth?

Customer: Account ending 7741, and my date of birth is March 15, 1985.

Agent: Thank you, Sandra. I have verified your identity. I can see there was an access attempt from an IP address in Phoenix, Arizona at 11:42 AM today. Your account in our system shows your usual location as Seattle. I am immediately terminating all active sessions and locking your account.

Customer: Did they access anything? Can they see my money?

Agent: The login attempt failed our security check because of the unusual location, so your account was not accessed. Your funds are safe. However, as a precaution I am flagging this and escalating to our fraud team.

Customer: Thank goodness. What do I do now?

Agent: I will send a password reset link to your registered email address right now. Please do not use your old password. I also strongly recommend enabling two-factor authentication — I can walk you through that if you would like. Additionally, please check if you use the same password on any other sites and change those too.

Customer: Yes, please walk me through the two-factor setup.

Agent: Of course. Once you reset your password and log back in, go to Security Settings, then select Two-Factor Authentication and choose SMS or an authenticator app. I will stay on the line to confirm it works.

Customer: Okay the reset link just arrived... done. I am setting up SMS verification now. It is asking for a code... got it, it is confirmed.

Agent: Excellent. Your account is now secured with two-factor authentication. The fraud team will review the unauthorized attempt and may contact you in the next 24 hours. Is there anything else I can help with?

Customer: No, I feel much better now. Thank you for acting so quickly.

Agent: Absolutely. Your security is our priority. Please call us any time you see something unusual. Stay safe, Sandra.
